name: Sourdough Pizza Dough
summary: A chewy, blistered sourdough pizza base with a long cold ferment for flavor.
tips: For extra chew and flavor, use a 50/50 mix of bread flour and 00 flour.
If the dough resists stretching, let it rest for 10 minutes and try again — it relaxes and gets easier.
You can freeze dough balls raw after the cold ferment — just thaw overnight in the fridge before use.

steps.title: Mix the Dough
steps.instruction: In a large bowl, mix the sourdough starter and lukewarm water until dissolved. Add the flour and mix with a spoon (or by hand) until no dry bits remain. Let rest for 30 minutes (autolyse).

steps.title: Add Salt and Oil
steps.instruction: Sprinkle the salt evenly and drizzle the olive oil over the dough. Mix well by folding the dough until both salt and oil are fully incorporated.

steps.title: Stretch & Folds
steps.instruction: During roughly the first 2 hours, perform 2–3 sets of stretch and folds — one set every 30–45 minutes — to build gluten strength. Reset the timer after each fold.
steps.tip: Keep the room around 70–75°F / 21–24°C for an even ferment.

steps.title: Bulk Fermentation
steps.instruction: After your folds, let the dough continue at room temperature until puffy and risen by about half — roughly 4–6 hours total from mixing.

steps.title: Cold Ferment (Optional but Recommended)
steps.instruction: After bulk fermentation, divide the dough into 2–3 portions. Shape into balls and place in lightly oiled containers or dough boxes. Cover and refrigerate for 12–48 hours — this develops flavor and makes the dough easier to stretch.
steps.tip: Longer cold ferment = more flavor. 24 hours is a great sweet spot.

steps.title: Bring to Room Temperature
steps.instruction: Take the dough out 1–2 hours before baking to come to room temperature and relax.

steps.title: Shape and Bake
steps.instruction: Preheat your oven to its highest temperature (ideally 475–500°F / 245–260°C) with a pizza stone or steel inside if possible. Lightly flour your surface, stretch each dough ball into a pizza round, and add your favorite toppings. Bake for 7–10 minutes until golden and bubbly.
